what_it_does: React implementation of the Modal dialog using React.createElement (aliased h). Renders nothing when open is false; when open, renders a .cv-modal-overlay containing a .cv-modal panel (role=dialog, aria-modal=true) with an optional head (title h2 + close button), the children body, and an optional footer. Adds a keydown listener that calls onClose on Escape, and closes on backdrop click (target === currentTarget).
observations: useEffect registers a document keydown listener only while open && onClose, cleaning it up on unmount/dependency change.
Returns null when !open (matches the .d.ts JSX.Element | null return).
Backdrop click handler only fires onClose when e.target === e.currentTarget (click on overlay, not the panel).
Head renders only if title or onClose exists; when no title but a close button exists, a flex:1 spacer span pushes the close button right.
Sets role='dialog' and aria-modal='true' on the panel; close button has aria-label 'Close' and renders a '×' glyph.
Uses inline style { flex: 1 } on the spacer span — a small inline-style literal.
Token-class wrapper over .cv-modal-overlay/.cv-modal/.cv-modal__head/__title/__close/__foot; no JSX, global React.
standouts: Backdrop-only close via target===currentTarget guard (clicks inside the panel don't dismiss).
Escape handling scoped to open && onClose with proper listener cleanup.
conventions: Class-driven styling referencing design-system modal classes (look lives in CSS) — matches single-source directive.
createElement instead of JSX; consumes a global React.
Accessibility: role=dialog, aria-modal, aria-label on close.
One inline-style literal ({flex:1}) on the spacer breaks the otherwise class-only styling.
concerns: No focus trap or focus restoration / portal — focus management is not handled here.
Inline style { flex: 1 } is a styling literal in a component meant to be class-driven.
notes: The Modal dialog runtime: a controlled overlay+panel class wrapper with Escape and backdrop-click close, ARIA dialog roles, and optional head/footer; renders null when closed.
questions: Is the absence of a focus trap / portal intentional, or handled elsewhere?
Where are the .cv-modal* classes defined (referenced, not in this file)?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — header comment describes a controlled token-class modal that closes on backdrop/Escape/button and renders nothing when closed; the code implements exactly that and matches its .d.ts.
apparent_themes: Controlled overlay with multiple close affordances
intention_signals: Aims to provide a minimal controlled modal whose appearance lives entirely in design-system classes, with conventional close behaviour and basic ARIA, without reimplementing styling.
contribution: Contributes the modal runtime — overlay/panel structure, Escape + backdrop-click close logic with listener cleanup, optional head/footer assembly, and the dialog ARIA wiring.
summary_for_embedding: React Modal component built with React.createElement that renders a controlled .cv-modal-overlay/.cv-modal dialog with title, children, and footer, closing on Escape, backdrop click, or a close button, and rendering null when closed.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a controlled, class-styled modal dialog with conventional close affordances and basic ARIA in the design system.
